titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the William Zeisl Papers LSC.2371
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Jasmine Jones, 2019 and Catherine Falls, 2026.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-26 11:26:58 -0700.

physdesc.extent: (3 unprocessed film strips, 3 unprocessed open reel audiotapes, 10 unprocessed records)

did.langmaterial: Materials are primarily in English. Some materials in Hebrew, Italian and German.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Unprocessed collection. Material is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Provenance/Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Walter and Betty Shavinsky Zeisl, 2018.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], William Zeisl papers (Collection 2371). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Potentially Offensive Description in Library Special Collections.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William Zeisl was born on January 24, 1907 in Vienna, Austria. After completion of high school and business college, he received a scholarship at the Vienna States Academy of Music as a master student. He worked as a soloist at the Vienna Radio Station and also sang as a soloist and member of a choir in Viennese Jewish Orthodox temples. He arrived in the United States in September, 1939 living in New York one year, and in Milwaukee, WI two years where he sang in several large synagogues as Cantor for the High Holidays. In 1942, he joined the U.S. Army and was discharged in August 1945, after which he moved to Los Angeles.
In 1944 he became the Cantor at the Fairfax Temple with Rabbi Jacob Sonderling and remained there for eleven years until September 1955. For the High Holidays he sang at the Unitarian Auditorium.
From 1955 through 1957 he sang at the Sinai Temple, Glendale where he was Cantor, and taught Sunday and Hebrew school.
He studied at Hebrew Union College Jewish Institute of Religion to become a certified Cantor on May 23, 1957.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection contains photographs, analog sound recordings, analog moving image, papers, monographs pertaining to the life and career of Cantor William Zeisl, brother of composer Eric Zeisl. Audiovisual and digital materials consist of 3 open reel audiotapes, 3 film strips, 10 records and 1 optical disc, and contain recordings of Zeisl's performances.
